data.author: Daniel
data.message:
drop KDL dependency, find joint limits & tcp orientation from RobotModel

Nice with fewer dependencies, but I'm unsure if RobotModel guarantees
that joint_states appear in topological order. If not, we're at the
mercy of the moveit config to define them as such. If it doesn't, joint
limits will be applied to the wrong joints.

data.author: Daniel
data.message:
automatically deduce tcp orientation (T_rostool_pendanttool)

This gets rid of the hardcoded T_rostool_pendanttool which assumed
the URDF / planning group matches the official FANUC URDF.

Now it's automatically deduced, meaning we can support any tool
orientation (e.g. paolofrance/ros2_fanuc_interface).

Finally, also extend tests a bit.

data.author: Daniel
data.message: micro-optimization

data.author: Daniel
data.message:
check orientation as well when confirming IK sol reproduces desired pose

Might revert this pending some quantative investigation on whether
there's ever a case where orientation is bad but position isn't.
Those 3x3 -> quaternion conversions are unfortunate ...

data.author: Daniel
data.message:
add randomized ik/fk roundtrip tests, fix some stuff along the way

specifically
  - dual solutions when not coupling J2/J3 (plugin behavior) would not
    be harmonized around +-180 for larger joint angles
  - put a bandaid (at the plugin level) on the fact that joint solutions
    can sometimes have a position error of several cm (after FK). This
    ensures no such solutions are returned.

implement plugin FK, add basic plugin test for CRX10ia and CRX30ia

Notably, the test does not require external repositories. The trade-off
is ~800 lines of SRDF and xacro-parsed URDF files being committed into
this repo, though.
